title: CGEL Attestation (Phase 3)
description: Sanitized, exportable task summary: ids, statuses, digests. No raw command output, no logs, no secrets. Persistence: `local` only — the attestation is written to the runtime state store and never committed. An enum of local|ci-artifact|pr-comment|repository was once advertised here and only `local` was ever implemented; `cgel attest` now rejects the other three rather than accepting them and printing a footnote. This schema has no additionalProperties: false and CGEL ships no jsonschema dependency, so it DOCUMENTS the attestation rather than constraining it. The tests assert presence and type by hand.

terminal_reason.description: The --reason given at close, verbatim. Required by the CLI for every terminal status; absent here only on an attestation built for an OPEN task (`cgel attest`).

pass_blockers.description: What the PASS validator would have objected to. Empty on a PASS (it could not have closed otherwise). On an ESCALATE or ABORT this is the useful part: what CGEL could NOT have certified.

user_sentence.description: The plain sentence CGEL asked the model to relay to the user. GUIDANCE_ONLY: recorded here so the record shows what the user should have been told, whether or not they were.

degradations.description: Controls that were not fully running for this task (e.g. 'workspace-binding-inert: no-git'). Empty when clean. A degradation does NOT gate PASS: the point is to kill the silence, not to add a refusal the user cannot act on.
